ai_enrichment.summary: The Windows registry utility reg.exe was used to query the Winlogon registry key, which contains auto-start programs and authentication settings. This activity was spawned by a PowerShell script explicitly named after the MITRE T1012 technique running from an Atomic Red Team directory, indicating deliberate adversary simulation or red team testing. Attackers query this key to identify persistence opportunities and credential handling configurations.
investigation_steps: 1. Confirm whether Atomic Red Team testing is authorized and scheduled for this endpoint at this time
2. Review full contents of T1012.ps1 script to identify all registry queries and any additional malicious actions
3. Query SIEM for all events from this host in the past hour with parent process containing 'AtomicRedTeam'
4. Check if labuser account is authorized for security testing activities
5. Search for other Discovery techniques (T1082, T1083, T1016) executed in close temporal proximity
6. Verify how AtomicRedTeam framework was deployed to this endpoint and by whom
containment_actions: If unauthorized: Immediately isolate host from network
If unauthorized: Disable labuser account pending investigation
If authorized testing: Document in change management and continue monitoring
Block execution of scripts from AtomicRedTeam directory if not part of approved testing
ai_enrichment.false_positive_reasoning: The explicit Atomic Red Team directory path and T1012-named script strongly suggest this is authorized adversary emulation testing rather than actual malicious activity.
